recommendations: deduplicate and namespace MCP endpoints to avoid collisions (e.g.
`forecasting.fit`
`transform.fit`
`classification.fit`)
add a generated endpoint manifest with signatures/docstrings and stability tags
implement strict Pydantic request/response schemas for each endpoint (including forecast horizon and index types)
add contract tests for every exposed endpoint (happy path
bad input
optional dependency missing
serialization round-trip)
create a minimal CI workflow for `mcp_output` (lint
type-check
smoke import
endpoint tests)
pin and align plugin dependencies with core sktime compatibility matrix (Python/scikit-learn/pandas ranges)
add graceful optional-dependency guards with actionable install hints for torch/tensorflow/prophet/etc.
introduce endpoint-level timeout/retry/error normalization in `mcp_service.py`
cache `all_estimators` and dataset metadata lookups to reduce cold-start latency
add structured logging and request tracing (endpoint
duration
error class)
provide a small set of high-value composite endpoints (`train_forecaster`
`evaluate_forecaster`
`load_split_fit_predict`) to reduce multi-call orchestration burden
validate and sanitize dynamic import targets to reduce import-mode security risk
add versioned API prefixing and backward-compat deprecation policy for MCP methods
include reproducibility controls (global random_state propagation and deterministic mode docs)
expand `README_MCP.md` with concrete end-to-end examples for forecasting/classification/detection
add performance smoke benchmarks (fit/predict latency and memory on `load_airline`/`load_gunpoint`) and track regressions
create fallback black-box execution path tests even when import mode is primary
expose health/readiness endpoints that verify critical optional backends availability
enforce response size limits/pagination for large outputs (e.g.
estimator registry)
add mypy/ruff/pre-commit configuration inside `mcp_output` to keep generated plugin maintainable
add golden tests for endpoint names to prevent accidental breaking renames

success_factors: Workflow reached terminal success state and executed all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Resilience mechanism worked: clone failure was recovered via zip fallback, enabling repository analysis (1612 files) and plugin generation.
Generated MCP service booted healthy over stdio and passed plugin-level health checks.
Low intrusiveness import-mode adapter selected with high import feasibility (0.9), appropriate for sktime’s API-heavy architecture.
failure_reasons: Primary git clone failed due to network/RPC timeout (curl 56, expected flush after ref listing).
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external semantic enrichment.
Execution telemetry quality issues: total_files_processed=0 and many zeroed metrics despite successful zip-based analysis/generation.
Original project tests were not actually validated (passed=false with empty details, execution_time=0), so upstream compatibility is unproven.

node_performance.download_time: Download node partially failed (git RPC timeout) but fallback succeeded; likely dominant source of instability and delay.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed with substantial static scan (1612 files) and useful module extraction; runtime appears reasonable for repo size.
node_performance.generation_time: Generation succeeded quickly with standard scaffold files, but quality signals are limited (LOC/file size reported as 0).
node_performance.test_time: Only MCP health/smoke checks effectively passed; no meaningful original-project or endpoint contract test timing captured.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient data (reported 0 MB likely telemetry gap).
resource_usage.disk_usage: Moderate expected: large repo processed plus generated MCP artifacts; actual measured size unavailable due to metric gaps.
